title: Bridge Paper: Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Metacognition as the 'diagnostic' capacity — science of knowing what you know, parallel to esoteric self-examination.

Paper: Neural Correlates of Metacognition Impairment in Opioid Addiction.
DOI: 10.1016/j.bpsc.2024.07.014
Abstract: BACKGROUND: Individuals with substance use disorder show impaired self-awareness of ongoing behavior. This deficit suggests problems with metacognition, which has been operationalized in the cognitive neuroscience literature as the ability to monitor and evaluate the success of one's own cognition and behavior. However, the neural mechanisms of metacognition have not been characterized in a population with drug addiction. Individuals with substance use disorder show impaired self-awareness of ongoing behavior. This deficit suggests problems with metacognition, which has been operationalized in the cognitive neuroscience literature as the ability to monitor and evaluate the success of one's own cognition and behavior. However, the neural mechanisms of metacognition have not been characterized in a population with drug addiction. METHODS: Community samples of participants with opioid use disorder (OUD) (n = 27) and healthy control participants (n = 29) performed a previously validated functional magnetic resonance imaging metacognition task (perceptual decision-making task along with confidence ratings of performance). Measures of recent drug use and addiction severity were also acquired.
